title: Youth Work Associate (Fixed Term, Up to Dec 2026)
description:
Job Description

Youth Work Associate (Fixed Term, Up to Dec 2026)

Position Type: Contract | Experience: 1–2 Years | Sector: Youth Services / Education Support

Key Focus: School-based Intervention, Mentoring, Life-Skills Facilitation, Stakeholder Engagement.

Job Description:

The Youth Work Associate (School-based) is stationed within partner secondary schools to provide frontline support for at-risk students (aged 10–16). Grounded in the Positive Youth Development (PYD) framework, the incumbent facilitates social-emotional learning, conducts small to large -group interventions, and serves as a bridging mentor between the school environment and the community.

School-based Engagement & Case Support

• Engagement: Build rapport with students through regular presence during in-class curriculum activity and school clubhouse.

• Engagement: Proficient in using creative arts, sports, or digital media to connect with hard-to-reach youths.

• Screening & Intake: Assist in identifying students with socio-emotional needs through school referrals and conducting initial screenings.

• Supportive Mentoring: Provide one-on-one "check-in" sessions to monitor student well-being and progress toward personal goals.

• Crisis Support: Provide immediate emotional first aid for students in distress under the guidance of the School Counsellor or CARE Team Supervisor.

____________________________________________________________________________________________

Programme Facilitation & Delivery

• Life-Skills Training: Support the delivery of structured workshops (e.g., self-management, responsible decision-making, and resilience) to classes or small groups.

• Group work: Facilitate group sessions that promote the "6 Cs" (Competence, Confidence, Connection, Character, Caring, Contribution).

• Experiential Learning: Assist in organizing and leading school-based excursions and holiday engagements that promote pro-social behaviour.

____________________________________________________________________________________________

Stakeholder Collaboration

• School Partnership: Work closely with Student Management Teams, Year Heads, and School Counsellors to align CARE’s interventions with the school’s discipline and pastoral care goals.
• Volunteer Support: Assist in liaising and coordination of volunteers/sponsors during school-based activities.

____________________________________________________________________________________________

Administration and Impact Measurement

• Data Management: Maintain accurate and timely case notes and attendance records for all school sessions.

• Outcome Evaluation: Administer pre- and post-programme assessments (e.g., Programme Evaluation System - PES) to track student development milestones.

• Reporting: Assist in preparing semester-based progress reports for school leadership.

____________________________________________________________________________________________

Professional Qualifications

• Diploma in Social Work, Youth Work, Counselling, Psychology, or a related Social Science discipline.

• 1–2 years of experience working with at-risk youth, preferably within school settings.

________________________________________

Technical & Soft Skills:

• Facilitation: Strong ability to manage group dynamics and engage groups of up to 40 participants.

• Communication: Fluency in English (written and spoken). Ability to speak a second language is highly advantageous for communicating with caregivers.

• Digital Literacy: Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and engagement tools (e.g., Canva, Mentimeter, Ahaslides).

________________________________________

Personal Attributes

• A strong passion and desire to make a positive impact on youths and young people.

• The ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, particularly for event management.

• A commitment to continuous learning and a passion for serving community needs.

• Must be resilient and a dedicated team player.

• The role may require occasional work outside of standard hours.

Please mention your expected salary and availability.

*Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for an interview.

postedCompany.description:
We are a social service organisation specialising in engaging children and youths at risk of academic failure, emotional and social maladjustment, and juvenile delinquency.

We offer school-based social work services as well as community-based youth engagement programmes.

By set-up, we are a registered society and voluntary welfare organisation with Institutions of a Public Character charity status.
postedCompany.name: Children-At-Risk Empowerment Association
